note_data.title: Statistics
keyPoints: **Data collection** methods include surveys, questionnaires, observations and experiments to gather primary data
**Frequency tables** organise raw data by counting how many times each value occurs
**Scatter diagrams** show the relationship between two variables with points plotted on x-y axes
**Mean** is the sum of all values divided by the number of values (for ungrouped data)
**Median** is the middle value when data is arranged in ascending order
**Mode** is the value that occurs most frequently in a data set
**Grouped data** uses class intervals when dealing with large ranges of continuous data
**Estimated mean** for grouped data uses midpoint of each class interval multiplied by frequency
**Modal class** is the class interval with the highest frequency in grouped data
**Correlation** describes the relationship shown in scatter diagrams as positive, negative or no correlation

definitions.definition: Data collected directly by the person conducting the investigation
The number of times a particular value or class interval occurs
A range of values used to group continuous data
A measure of how closely two variables are related
A value that lies outside the general pattern of the data

howToAnswer.instruction: Show clear working for mean/median/mode with correct formula substitution
Use midpoints for grouped data and state this is an estimate
State the type and strength of correlation from scatter diagram
Plot points accurately and label axes with units

modelAnswer.question:
The table shows the heights of students in a class in Karachi. Calculate an estimate of the mean height.

Height (cm) | 150-154 | 155-159 | 160-164 | 165-169
Frequency   |    3    |    7    |    5    |    2
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Correct sum and total frequency

commonMistakes: Never use class boundaries instead of midpoints for estimated mean calculations
Students often forget to arrange data in order before finding the median
Never say 'highest bar' for mode - always say 'highest frequency'
Students confuse modal class with actual mode in grouped data
Never draw correlation lines beyond the plotted data points
examinerTips: Statistics appears in every Paper 2 - usually worth 15-20 marks total
Always show midpoint calculations clearly for grouped data
Label axes and plot points accurately on scatter diagrams
State clearly whether answers are estimates for grouped data
formulas: Mean = Sum of values ÷ Number of values
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Scatter diagram with clearly labelled x and y axes, accurately plotted points, and line of best fit if correlation exists. Include title and units on both axes.
